What was supposed to be a luxury Carnival cruise vacation quickly turned into one of the worst cruise disasters in history. In 2013, the Carnival Triumph suffered an engine fire that left the ship stranded in the Gulf of Mexico for days with over 4,000 passengers and crew on board. With no power, no air conditioning, and overflowing toilets, the media dubbed it “The Poop Cruise From Hell.”
